question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TokenAirdropRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Size(max=2) constraint on receiverIds/senderIds/tokenIds/serialNumbers parameters in the controller, by supplying exactly 2 parameters with conflicting operators (e.g., gt:100 and gt:200) to bypass the verifySingleOccurrence() check if not called, generating an ambiguous SQL condition that returns fee amounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TokenAirdropRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that @Size(max=2) allows exactly 2 range parameters, by supplying two EQ operators for the same field (e.g., tokenId=eq:100&tokenId=eq:200) to cause the Bound to hold two lower bounds (both EQ treated as lower), generating a contradictory SQL condition that returns unexpected fee-bearing records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TokenAirdropRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cardinality tracking in Bound which uses Math.addExact() for merging, by supplying more than Integer.MAX_VALUE occurrences of the same operator (if the @Size constraint is bypassed), causing an ArithmeticException that leaks internal stack trace information about fee data structures?
